74744.subject: 📉 Warsh crashes markets in first FOMC
74744.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $63,907 (-1.4%) | **ETH** $1,742 (-1.2%) | **SOL** $71 (-1.5%)

* US and Iran officially [sign MOU](https://x.com/BRICSinfo/status/2067358549777248417?s=20) to end the war

* Fed holds rates steady and omits forward guidance at Warsh's [first meeting](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2067307283919581688) — Odds of a July [rate hike](https://polymarket.com/event/fed-decision-in-july-181) increase to 17%

* ==Stocks ====[lost ](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2067332084188737900)==[$1.2T](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2067332084188737900)== in market cap while Bitcoin fell to $64K==

* STRC hits a [record low](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2067419202819109018?s=20) as concerns grow that Strategy may need to [sell more Bitcoin](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2067203359292219443?s=20) to fund dividends

* AI image and video generation company Midjourney reveals [full body medical scanner](https://x.com/midjourney/status/2067421950314688759?s=20)

* ==Coinbase launches SEC-registered ====[AI investment advisors](https://x.com/coinbase/status/2066969129110630667)==

* ==CME group plans to ====[sue the CFTC](https://x.com/fintechfrank/status/2067422497998315931?s=20)==== over regulation of perpetual futures==

* ==France plans to phase out securities that lack ==quantum encryption== amid growing ====[Bitcoin security worries](https://decrypt.co/371487/france-phase-out-non-quantum-encryption-bitcoin-security-concerns-grow)==

* ==US House Representative says Congress is ready to ====[fast track the Clarity Act](https://x.com/Cointelegraph/status/2067477441455493599?s=20)==

* ==White House demands that Fable 5 be ====[impossible to jailbreak](https://x.com/Polymarket/status/2067357928206594261?s=20)==== before re-release — Anthropic CEO says companies testing Mythos liken it to a ====[super weapon](https://x.com/Polymarket/status/2067339660905365935?s=20)==== that should “require a gun license”==

* ==Ansem and Arthur Hayes tease an ====[upcoming memecoin launch](https://x.com/CryptoHayes/status/2067407397480808895?s=20)==== on Solana==

* ==Nvidia builds ====[self-training](https://decrypt.co/371456/nvidia-built-robots-train-themselves-ai-coding-agents)==== AI powered robots==

Fear and Greed is at
21
(Fear).
BTC slipped to
$64K
after the Fed held rates with a hawkish tilt yesterday.

The Fed gave its answer. Now state-level policy is making its
own move. A new crypto tax just became law in Illinois. Senate
lawmakers formally opposed the SBF pardon. And Solana just hit a
milestone no other public blockchain has reached.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

The Fed Just Gave Crypto a Hawkish Signal

The Federal Reserve held rates steady on June 17 in Kevin
Warsh's first meeting as chair, but raised its 2026 rate forecast
and flagged Middle East uncertainty. BTC fell to $64,800 before
recovering.

ETH gained 7.6% and SOL rose 13% over the week despite the macro
pressure.

What did the Fed cite as the key factors keeping inflation above
its 2% target?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/kHmFp9JYSrdg3T_GD-H7Lw~~/AAQRxRA~/m-3fBG0oizvwC5f9YBt8tmzwoi7krHbxfOtwkLMiZRk0o_ih9jDZHYdmsJCj17UYNPRz6eSgvm8LZaztT9PdFIKQ3-qjsjn-dQFBWt0GaUuQBvU7FtgNtaykhsIL5JCp7D92Y4U-BiOPGDFf8WXvKQXFL1rI1wBx0or3Bb62puE~ )
   Senate Just Moved Against SBF's Pardon Bid

Senators Cynthia Lummis and Ruben Gallego introduced a formal
resolution opposing any clemency for Sam Bankman-Fried this week,
putting Congress on record against the pardon petition he filed
this month. The resolution is non-binding.

The Second Circuit upheld his conviction days earlier, leaving
him ine

74739.subject: Make AI Run Your Workflow
74739.body:
AI NOW & BEYOND

Автор: AI NOW & BEYOND


Автор: AI NOW & BEYOND

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/make-ai-run-your-workflow-gptai-z7fyc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B9ANb97FOQpSnzOVcgJuzVg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2i_RGPBIJl8sk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqje2zou~5s-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqje2zou-5s
  
Today in AI NOW & BEYOND 🔥 Turn AI from a Tool into a Business Process Anthropic Suspends Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After U.S. Export Control Order AI Resources Trending AI Tools Turn AI from a Tool into a Business Process Sponsored The real cost of AI isn't the subscription. It's the hours spent repeating instructions, rebuilding workflows, and starting from scratch every time. Whether you're researching ideas or analyzing information, the biggest gains come when AI becomes part of a repeatable process. Learn from Sam Keen, former engineer at AWS, Nike, and Lululemon, how to build reliable workflows with Claude Code that save time and deliver more consistent results. Get 60% off with code AI60. First 10 sign-ups only. Sign-up at 60% Off Anthropic Suspends Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After U.S. Export Control Order Company disables access globally after national security directive raises concerns over potential jailbreak risk Anthropic temporarily suspended Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after a US export control order raised national security concerns. The directive requires blocking foreign nationals, so Anthropic disabled both models for all customers while it works on compliance. Key Points: Models pulled globally: Anthropic disabled access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers after receiving the directive. National security concern: The government letter reportedly did not provide details, but Anthropic believes the concern may involve a possible method to jailbreak Fable 5. Foreign national restriction: The order blocks access by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the U.S., including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Older models unaffected: Existing Anthropic models like Opus and Sonnet remain available and are not covered by the directive. Anthropic pushes back: The company argues that a narrow potential jailbreak should not justify recalling a commercial model used by hundreds of millions of people. Safeguards already tested: Anthropic says Fable 5 underwent extensive red-teaming with U.S. and U.K. government partners, private organizations, and internal teams, and that no universal jailbreak has been found. Why It Matters: This is a major escalation in government control over frontier AI. If export officials can force labs to pull models over jailbreak risks, companies may face stricter rules for releasing advanced AI, especially in c
